lines.x: 11:37 P.M. \ Cloudy
Lungmen, Sunset Boulevard, a certain bar, the last unwiped table near the back
Jack of Spades. Straight flush. I win again, Gambino.
You win again. But you deal slower than I do with my toes.
You backin' out again?
Shut up.
Shut up? Wish you knew how to shut up last night. Coulda saved us a lotta trouble.
You been in Lungmen a long time, Capone. Maybe you need a reminder in how you talk to me, the boss.
Sit down, you moron. It's thanks to that temper of yours that business in Siracusa's so lousy.
You been here seven or eight years, and what've you done besides learn some Lungmen swearings? You stood here and watched our channels dry up?
I'm the one who got us a supporter last night. Try as you did to cock it up.
And besides, here in Lungmen, you and me are equals, 'boss.'
The way this Rat King runs the show here, ain't it just the old worn out Siracusan tricks?
If only you could play a good Siracusan hand worth a damn.
Too bad you're the worst card in it.
We got a role to play in Lungmen. Think about it: we got a tired, broken down city with a rich underworld. It could be a new home for the Family.
Only Siracusa can truly respect us, Capone. Don't forget the shame of being driven out of our home with our tails between our legs.
You can't eat honor.
Hmph.
Wei Yenwu set some rules for this city. As long as we don't break 'em, we can run our rackets here just like before. Lungmen needs a little 'legitimate business.'
...I ain't seen you in years and I'm already gettin' sick of you.
Same same, boss.
You...!
Can you not have your noisy lovers' quarrel in here? This lousy champagne's doin' enough to piss me off. Grab a bottle and take it outside, aight?
Get outta here. This don't concern you.
Watch your tone. I'm the boss in here.
We got a one-stop funeral service, if you're interested. 20% off if you both die together.
Hey... ain't this guy...?
...This is what we get since we left Siracusa. Nobody knows you're Sicilian.
Who gives a shit who you are?
Yo, Capone, do me a favor and torch this tacky joint. Call it 'urban beautification.' Wei Yenwu'll thank us for tearin' down a tastelessly decorated rathole.
Quiet! Don't provoke him!
Oh?
Who you callin' 'tasteless?'
6:44 P.M. \ Clear
Lungmen Ring Expressway, Car
Young Master?
Young Master, are you asleep?
Hm, oh... Where are we?
We will soon arrive at the rendezvous point. Do clear your head, Young Master. Penguin Logistics is already there waiting for you.
Mm. Right.
You seem exhausted.
I'm fine.
Forgive my indiscretion, Sir, but you do seem to have made this decision in some haste. You know of course that you may at any time share any matters that trouble you.
...My father... he definitely has his own ideas.
But all I have to do is get Penguin Logistics on board. Then those guys won't be able to get between me and my father ever again.
You are the youngest Messenger in the history of your family, Young Master. You perform all of your duties impeccably. Surely that will be enough.
Maybe. But who knows if they'll feel the same way.
Young Master...
Looking out at the city, all I can think about is how almost all of Lungmen's Messenger firms belong to my father.
Penguin Logistics is the last. And the most independent. And the one with a ton of weird rumors swirling around it.
My father gets along pretty well with Mr. Emperor, but we have to do more to really get them. Or at least I do.
That is why I worry, Young Master. I am well aware Penguin Logistics is a most peculiar organization.
Don't look at me like that. I got this.
You might change your expression as well, Young Master. Perhaps if you smiled more, like the boys your age.
It's Sauin tonight. You could go out on the town with your friends.
...real funny. Me not having friends is a joke to you, huh?
Heavens no! Perish the thought! Haha.
Dreadfully sorry, Young Master. Do you have your shield with you?
What's up?
I'm afraid we're being watched.
Target sighted. You ready?
Gah, this fuse is still all...
Just do it already.
Nng... an explosion?!
Hold on, Young Master!
Target sighted. He's still up.
Bring him in. Quick.
(They bombed out the road...? But who...?)
(Shit... my vision's... all blurry...)
Report.
Lots of witnesses, but I don't see the other targets.
Hold up, there's somebody in the smoke there!
Another survivor? You gotta-
Nnng...
Hey? Hey!
You got whacked that easy?
Talk about a hackneyed mob movie plot,
Penguin Logistics.
Ooh, sounds like you passed Screenwriting 101.
But like, throwing up a roadblock on a suburban highway and burying a bomb on the side there. Isn't that also really cliche?
Demolition's just a hobby of mine. Take a look around and I think you'll find things ain't so simple.
Damn. How much Originium did that mook use in his bomb? The Rat King'll have our balls for breakfast if he finds out.
We found a Penguin Logistics Messenger. It's Exusiai. Looks like she bumped off the wrecking crew...
Do I look blind to you? Where's the wolf? Texas?
Dunno. Don't see her.
Fuggedaboutit. Surround 'em. Let's get this done.
An ambush, huh? They're nothing if not thorough.
Hey, can you move? Your limbs are all shaky.
Yeah, kinda... You must be... Exusiai?
That's me! And you're... uh... Whatsisname!
Bison.
We... we should get out of here.
Before you get all mopey, that old butlery guy is a-okay.
Really?!
I put him by the roadside for a minute, but he doesn't look like he needs much help... anyway!
Exusiai, Messenger of Penguin Logistics, and the little rich kid there, give it up. Come with us nice and easy-like.
All these fresh faces, must be your relatives from Siracusa? Didn't you teach them how things work in Lungmen?
None of your damned business. We're cleaning you out for good this time.
Oh come on... You've been in Lungmen so long you turned basic. Grabbing territory like a common thug.
I guess at least some of the Siracusan Mafia can do more than just bar brawls.
Grr... I'll teach you to look down on a Sicilian. *Lungmen Swearing* Get 'em!
Awright, looks like I'm right on time!
Croissant, you made it!
But they're all so ornery. What're we gonna do?
The usual. You clear a path, I'll bring up the rear, we split the completion bonus.
Now that's all ya had to say! Yeeehawww!
Th- that crazy broad charged right through!
...get after them. But not too close. Wait for backup.
Got it!
You lost 'em, cazzo.
Look, I ain't a kidnapping expert. The rest of Penguin Logistics never showed and we weren't about to go traipsing into downtown Lungmen by ourselves.
I don't wanna hear excuses. There's a car headed your way. Convertible. Old friends inside.
...already?
Gah, damn it. Another dead end?
It's a buncha old ruins nobody's touched fer ages. Lemme just make my own hole already.
H- hold up! Where are we going?
There they are! It's a dead end! We got 'em!
It's game over, isn't it?
Oh yeah!
You have an idea?!
We haven't properly introduced ourselves, have we?
Rub 'em out!
Now's not the time for this! We've got more important things to deal with!
Nah.
This is important. Real important.
We got a serious emphasis on corporate culture. And today's culture buzzword is 'courtesy.'
I just came up with it.
It's Emperor! Somebody call the boss!
There's someone else with him!
Came up empty, Exusiai.
Not every Siracusan mook's a total moron. We'll get 'em next time, don't worry.
But first, I've got to introduce you to Penguin Logistics's newest temp worker: Bison.
They blocked the only way out. We- we have to fight through!
Get down on the ground. You don't have to die.
Woah! Settle down, we just have to hold out until the boss...!
Hold out?
Hit the lights.
Agh! It's so bright! Where'd that spotlight come from?!
Welcome to Penguin Logistics, little doggies.
Now I can see you. And that means you're on land that belongs to the Penguin Empire.
Now I hate to bother you, but I'm gonna have to see your landing visa. You got it on you?
No?
Then I think we gotta send you crying home to your mamas.
7:10 P.M. \ Clear
Penguin Logistics Base, Messy Room
So this is where Penguin Logistics holes up...
(It's like a disaster area. And so dark.)
It's one of our bases. We haven't cleaned up in a while. Just sit wherever.
Oh, thanks... I realize I didn't properly thank you, any of you.
We did our jobs. You should introduce yourself first.
I'm a Messenger, codenamed Bison. I'm from Lungmen, and I work for Mountain Dash Logistics. My father sent me to learn what I can from your company. And I appreciate the opportunity.
Now about the contract between me and Mr. Emperor, I know we've already been over it, but...
Hold up, little man, I got a question. How'd your bigass daddy raise his boy to be so...
...Boss.
Yeah yeah, go ahead.
Anyway, about that ambush, it was definitely meant to provoke a reaction from MDL and Penguin Logistics.
We can't take this sitting down. I can contact my father and the LGD. This is a case of aggravated assault...
Hey, Texas.
Hm?
What's for dinner?
We're throwing a welcome party, of course! But where's Sora? Didn't she stay home to hold down the fort?
Ya know she'll come a-runnin' soon as Texas gets on the horn.
Oh, sorry. You were sayin'?
(Sora?)
...Um, the first order of business is to figure out who attacked us. Does Penguin Logistics have any leads?
Leads? It's a pretty basic business dispute.
What do you mean, business dispute...?
Texas! Shouldn't I have a cigar in my hand by now?!
Boss, it's been a while since we shacked up here. I think all your smokes went bad.
Sora came here to clean before. She might have thrown them away.
Y'all for real? You tryin' to kill me? This is wack...
Yeah, you keep talkin.' Don't worry.
Our first step should be to figure out exactly what these attackers were targeting...
Hold up, we need some tunes! Get some vinyl spinnin'! We got a whole box of them here, don't we?
Purdy sure I hauled one over. A box worth a couple months' salary.
Found it. What do you want to hear?
Just throw on any one of 'em. Anything I picked out's gonna be fire.
It ain't life without music. Bless. Nice tunes, nice me.
Bison, what else you got?
...Oh. Where was I?
Their target.
Right! Well, their target was me, I guess, to try to provoke our two companies.
That's it? I figured it'd be some revenge thing...
So you have a lead?
Sigh. Texas! Go scout 'em out!
Triple overtime.
Um, we get into scuffles like that maybe twenty times a month. Doesn't every Messenger deal with this all the time?
...Why?
Messengers should be quieter, quicker... you shouldn't be settling everything with violence all the time.
Huh.
We're a logistics company. Why are we always getting involved in gang wars?
cause they got no taste. They don't value their own lives.
And the baws signs our checks. We still get the goods where they need to go, all nice an' legal-like.
It's just we usually end up doin' some armed transport.
Yeah. Like, what's the big deal?
Umm...? You're really okay with that?
Ugh...
Wait a minute.
Here, it's the passcode Sora left.
C- cookies?
Aww, why'd she open them and leave them here. They're no good when they get all soggy.
The chocolate side is long, and the tail is short. That's the passcode.
(That's some weird cryptography...)
Really? She wasn't kiddin' with that?
...All I did was memorize what Sora said.
'Suspect.' Hm. Maybe she chased them out.
Let her go. It's quittin' time.
And we got Sauin, so no overtime. Forget about capitalist oppression. We gotta pour one out for the homies.
Who's drinkin' with me?
Ooh, you buyin', baws? I heard some new top shelf booze just came in stock~
S'all good. You ladies got a lotta pay to dock, after all.
No thanks!
Wait! We... we never came up with a plan.
Don't need one.
We just let the mob get away with this?
Play it by ear.
...The LGD won't butt in?
Probably not. They're used to this stuff.
Ugh...
It is what it is. Just go with the flow.
But last month's property damage invoice was a little longer than usual. You all need to be more careful.
So what's your typical work like?
We deliver the cargo in accordance with the contract, and then we fight. But if we have to fight, that kinda takes precedence.
Oh and jobs go to the boss first. Then it's first come first serve, whoever can handle them.
Hey, little man. Your daddy trusted you to me. So you're part of the Penguin Logistics family now, even if it's just for a bit.
Er, yeah... I get it.
And you gotta remember the first rule of Penguin Logistics: 'Don't sweat the small stuff.'
Wasn't the first rule 'live for the party' yesterday?
The one I heard was 'carpe diem.'
...pretty much.
Those ain't gonna be the last soldiers who wanna scoop you up and haul you off, but we're gonna take 'em one at a time. Simple as that.
Hold up, why's my ass feel funny? Somebody leave something in my perfect, ergonomic recliner?
Um, I'll take a look.
Oh, what an adorable little candy box! Boss, you were hiding snacks under your chair!
That's cap. Why would I stuff snacks in my... did you say candy box?
It's a box of candies. See, it says 'Victorian Gumdrops' right here...
Get back, Exusiai, don't open the——
Huh?
Gah! Get down.
*Cough cough* Everybody okay?
Just managed to block it——
Nice reflexes, you two.
Whew, they really blew it up, didn't they?
————*indescribable penguin sounds*————
(Wh- what's that? Baws's makin' all kinds of crazy sounds!)
(Hey, wasn't that box of vinyl records some kind of black market Columbian collectible thingy?)
(I reckon yer right. What're we gonna do? I ain't never seen the baws all choked up like this before...)
What do you mean—— Hey! They're getting away! Those guys in black!
Wait! If they were here setting that trap, why didn't they just ambush us? This isn't adding up. We need to take a minute and come up with a plan before——
Texas!
Get in.
...Listen up.
You can expense your speeding tickets tonight.
They gotta pay... for my vinyl recoooords!!!
Got 'em! Dead ahead!
Exusiai! Pass me my Lil Homie!
You got it, boss!
Is that a gun?! But how are you going to pull the trigger...?
Hah! See for yourself, little man. Let our firearms expert tell you about it.
That's forty-two layers of logistics-grade cardboard, seamlessly glued together with industrial adhesive, driven by the finest rubber bands. It's really a magnificent piece.
You mean... it's a toy?
It's more of a slingshot——Ow! Err, it's a peacemaker!
You can't use live ammo in downtown Lungmen. That's the law.
And yet this is the only time the boss seems to care what the law says.
If nobody follows the rules, our whole society goes to hell, you feel me? Texas, put the top down!
Be careful. Don't want you hitting your head on a traffic signal like last time.
Do I look that tall to you?
Support sniper in position, boss!
W- wait a minute, there's a lot of other cars on the road. Don't tell me you're just gonna——
They're shootin' at us! Return fire!
How am I supposed to aim with you swervin' all over the place?!
Hey, are you hit back there?!
I'm bleeding, they got me, I need a bandage—— No wait, what is this, rubber?
A what?!
It's rubber, but it broke the glass! Shake 'em off, quick! Gah, that hurt!!
Clean headshot, boss!
Sic 'em, Texas!
I'm speeding up.
Slow down! Watch out for that big rig—— Aie!
Yer gonna wanna hold on tight. Texas's whipped up wilder'n a Forte in a twister right now.
Exu, blow their tires!
On it!
She's gonna pop their tires with rubber bullets?!
I have spoken. So let it be done.
Texas, can you talk some sense into them?!
(Looks away) ......
Forget it. They're havin' a good time.
Check this out! It's gonna do a cool flip!
Hey, I can't move the steering wheel!
Shit, bail out!
Wait! Going this fast, we're gonna get pulled in!
Hah.
All right, now the multi-car pileup is all yours, Texas!
Gee, thanks a lot.
7:16 P.M. \ Clear
Lungmen Central Park, Finball Stand
Your finballs.
Oooh, such springy mouthfeel. Pretty good.
This place definitely earned that recommendation in the Great Foodie Guide!
Glad you like it.
...That finball guy kinda gives me the creeps.
I know what you mean. Like, you're selling finballs, why do you have a big knife on the counter there?
The Foodie Guide says the owner is an old man called 'The Godly Finball Uncle.'
He couldn't be some kind of triad or something...
Hm? Did you want something else?
Oh, no.
...customers have been so weird lately.
I'm sure it's got nothing to do with that 'Resting Triad Face' you've got going on.
And this is what you wear to work? Think about how this makes Uncle Tung look.
Welcome back. Done with work?
You could say that. The office said you took some credit. How much?
32.60, rounded. You want to try one?
Where'd that come from? I don't have any change on me.
Consider it a token of my appreciation for doing so much to look after my business.
All right. Don't mind if I do.
Hmm...
It's tasty and all, but isn't this Uncle Tung's stall? A tribute made with borrowed goods is one without meaning.
You always have to get your digs in. This one's on me...
Okay then. You've definitely gotten better. I wish the other punks took things as seriously as you do.
Evening. I'll take some finballs.
Coming right up.
Take your time. I'm just enjoying the view of the river. Usually don't see it from this far away.
Smells nice. The Great Foodie Guide never gets it wrong.
...order up. Here's your change.
Thanks.
Please come again.
Waai Fu? What are you looking at?
It's just that girl kinda weirded me out... do Sankta grow horns?
Must be some Sauin makeup or something.
They looked real. Whatever. Idle gossip is an unbecoming habit.
What's next on the agenda? Back to the office?
Studying. I've got a test in two days.
You're spending Sauin studying?
Sauin is supposed to be about shepherding spirits into the afterlife. Our society has totally corrupted it with consumerism. Respectfully, I will abstain.
Not to mention, I don't get paid that much. I need to get my grades up and nab a scholarship.
Yeah, that sounds about right for you...
It's their fault for being so out of touch. Don't lump me in with them.
Hm, I've still got some time. Where should I go next? Let's see, looks like there's some good eats around here...
Woah! What's happening up on the overpass? A car crash?
Doesn't seem like a regular crash. Let's go check it out.
Another street fight? Count me in!
Friggin psychos, get away from meee!
Oh... so soon?
Looks like I'm on the clock already.
Ow... I was thrown clear...?
Thank heaven for guard rails. Otherwise we'd be a puddle down there.
Hurry up! Pull me out!
They're trying to get away! Stay on them!
Mmmff! This... airbag... it's smooshing me!
Hold still! You're gonna crush my Lil Homie! Yo! Croissant! It's comin' outta your pay!
It ain't me! I can't move at all with Texas on top of me!
Oof.
Then I guess it's up to me——!
Bison! Wait!
Everyone's crowding around. If they get away now we'll never catch them!
Pull me out already! That Forte kid's coming!
I'm trying!
Don't move!
So... heavy...
Who——
A sniper?!
Yo, Texas.
On it.
(They missed. Bad shot?)
(No, they're trying to hit the fuel tank——!)
Huh, what's going on? Who's shooting at the Forte kid?
Focus! The wolf's on her way too! Pull me out already!
Wait, what's that smell? It's fuel! Hurry!!
Brother... it's what it is.
You son of a——!
Damn! I can't——
Ugh. Too late.
The shots are coming from above? Are they up on the overpass?
How high is the angle of attack? Where could they be shooting from?
Doesn't matter. First I've got to——!
Nng——!
Oof!
I- I'm okay. What's this...? It's sticky...
A Sauin candle? Real lucky these were here...
You got one stuck in your horn too.
Huh? Oh, thanks. I guess I better yank it... out——!
Don't move, candle boy.
Quiet. Take cover behind that crate.
We lost Fronzo's signal. Oughta be around here somewhere!
Penguin Logistics is here too. Tell the boss as soon as you spot the target.
You can come out.
Oh, okay...
I appreciate your help, but you really don't wanna get mixed up in this. You better go somewhere safe.
I'd love to. It's been a while since I had a trip to Lungmen. But a job's a job...
Considering how I found you, I'm guessing your deal with Penguin Logistics isn't going so well?
...you could say that.
Err, who are you, exactly? I get the feeling you're not a typical passer-by.
Calm down. Let's just say I'm not your enemy.
Whoops, they're coming back. Hide.
Mamma mia! Those Penguin bastards got Fronzo! What're we gonna do?
That Bison kid's all alone. This could be our big break.
Can we really just yoink him in the middle of downtown Lungmen?
Mr. Capone says as long as we do it nice and quiet-like, hold off on the guns and bombs, Lungmen won't make a fuss.
Nobody's gonna go looking in those filthy old alleyways, eh?
Then we'll be the backup, you find the kid. Capiche?
Well, we can't stay here. Let's get going.
Let's see... over there. Let's say hello.
Ya find Bison yet, baws?
I'm wearin' shades, not binoculars. Gimme some space!
Aight... I see 'im. Waving at me.
Well let's go get him, then come back and ask this guy what they're up to.
Mmmf—— Mmmh——
Nah.
Looks like our lucky little Bison had a smooth landing. He can get hisself back here.
Um... he took a forty-foot drop onto a smooth landin'? How's that?
And the bad guys are kinda hunting for him. You sure we shouldn't go get him?
She's got him.
...She's back?
Exusiai, stay focused.
There they are! We gotta spring Fronzo!
Sigh. There's just no end to you people.
I think we lost them.
So what's Texas and the girls gotten themselves into this time? Who are they fighting now?
Can I really trust you?
I guess I better introduce myself. I'm Mostima. A Messenger for Penguin Logistics.
I think that makes me your colleague, for now. I usually work alone though.
...Mostima. I've heard that name from my father before.
(Not to mention a Sankta with black horns... the rumors were true. Better not ask about them though...)
Oh...? Good things I hope?
Erm... how should I say...? Just baseless rumors...
And after you saved me like that, I have no reason to doubt you. Sorry.
Don't worry about it. You got nothing to apologize for.
Thanks... Now we should share our intelligence... although I'm still pretty confused.
Basically, Penguin Logistics is in some kind of conflict with an arm of the Siracusan mafia.
Right. Got it.
Huh? You got it already?
I don't need any more. You need to stop thinking so much. Trust your instincts. That's the Penguin Logistics way.
Oh. So the trick is to just stop thinking, huh...?
They're a very special bunch. Common sense doesn't really work here.
...What about you, Mostima?
Are you always this direct with people?
I- I think so.
We need to get moving.
Follow me.
Eyo, you found the target yet?
There's too many people. Hey, I'm walkin' here!
C'mon, let's check somewhere else.
...I don't think they saw us.
Just keep going like this, and we'll figure out a way to meet back up with the others.
Right.
Hm.
Wh- what is it?
If we keep going like this, we'll end up...
G- getting caught by the mob?!
So that's where it was. It's been so many years since I've been back to Lungmen. All the signs are different.
Right... are you hungry? I'm trying to decide between matcha and chocolate.
Phew... It's getting late. Time to go home and sleep...
This is a great place to run a seafood stand. I can't believe how many customers came through. Hm? Is that...?
Hey.
I'll break this arm if I have to.
It's me, it's me! Let go!
Huh? What are you doing here?
I just closed up. I'm on my way home.
You're the one who's here spacing out—— Well, I guess it's not spacing out if you can flip around and snap my neck like that...
Did you hear that car accident just now, up on the overpass?
...aren't you supposed to be studying?
One must strike a balance between recreation and labor. If I spent the whole night before the exam cramming, I'd go in there with my brain totally fried.
Yeah, sure.
I heard some customers talking about it. I hope nobody died.
Same... but I heard something at the office. I guess there's some real unsavory characters mixed up in this...
Let's get this out of the way: I have nothing to do with any killers, gangs, or anyone in that world. You know that, right?
Everybody knows your deal—— Or maybe I should just say, with this attitude of yours, it's natural to be a little suspicious?
...right. I get it.
And there was a gas explosion on a highway in the outskirts just two hours ago.
A gas explosion on a highway? That's a pretty convenient explanation.
Hey, you're not thinking...?
I told you: one must strike a balance between recreation and labor.
...right. I get it.
So is this recreation or labor?
There we go. They can't do anything downtown like this. Too many people.
You ordered the matcha, right? This one's yours. My treat.
I don't see any mafia-looking guys, but... aren't we letting our guard down a bit too much?
What are you talking about? This place is a FIVE STAR recommendation. That's way more awesome than some mobsters.
You been able to contact Texas and them yet?
No, it was so fast...
So what's to be all nervous about?
Your ice cream's melting. You gotta calm down already.
Er, it's just... nobody could have predicted this. I should calm down.
These mobsters came aaall the way from Siracusa to Lungmen. We can bet they're not here for Sauin, right?
...They definitely have some other goal. One of them is me, but beyond that, I don't know.
We're in a good position. We managed to lose them. And with just the two of us versus however many of them, we have the advantage in scouting them out...
But how do we proceed? Should we start hunting them? No, the better move is to reconnect with Texas...
Heh. You seem a lot more like an ordinary Messenger than Texas and the girls.
Oh, sorry, was I talking to myself?
No, you're right.
We know the mob is up to something, we can't just go off half cocked.
But sometimes you gotta trust in the girls. I know they can be a little out there, but Penguin Logistics is a tough cookie.
It's kind of weird to call a logistics firm 'tough,' but... Forget it. It's not like I've got any room to complain.
Let's just take a walk, enjoy the scenery. And maybe by the time we run into Texas and the crew, they'll have this whole gang war all wrapped up?
...erm, really?
Ah...
Mostima?
You see that candy store?
It's really more of a stall than a store.
Hm, it is pretty old.
I came here on a trip to Lungmen years ago.
Back then... I didn't have any Lungmen dollars on me. Honestly, I must've looked a sight. But still, I found myself drawn to the candy.
O... kay.
That's what a Messenger does. We get into trouble on our long and arduous journeys.
C'mon, let's take a look.
Wow! It's a twinkly star!
Mister, how did you pull a star down here?
Sorry, but that's a secret I'll have to keep.
That's not a star! It's just a candy with some food coloring!
But I've never seen such a pretty candy before... you sure you don't want one?
Y- you're such a pain! Don't they have any cotton candy?
Of course we do. Here, give me your hand.
Oh... but I don't want cotton candy. I want that one...
I hate fruity candies! They're too sweet! And they turn your tongue weird colors!
Just take what you want. No charge. For Sauin.
Now it's getting late. You kids hurry home and get to bed!
Thanks, mister!

Cheers!!
